title: Medical Sounds
learningGoal: The learner will master the English pronunciation of critical medical vocabulary, specifically decoding the non-phonetic 'ache' /eɪk/ suffix in headache, stomachache, and toothache.
instructions: Medical words in English often have very strange spelling! The letters 'C-H' usually make a 'CH' sound, but in medical words, they make a 'K' sound. Practice saying these words perfectly so a doctor can understand you.

contentItems.text: The magic word today is ACHE. It means pain. It is spelled with 'C-H', but it is pronounced like the letter 'A' followed by the letter 'K'. A-K (एक).

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Sounds exactly like simply saying the letter A, then the letter K. A-K.
Warning! Stomach also ends with a 'K' sound! Stuh-mik-ayk.
Push your tongue against your teeth for the 'Th' before saying 'Ache'.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: दर्द करता है / चोट लगी है
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: A soft 'H' sound like blowing air. Hurts.
The 'tion' at the end makes a 'shun' sound.
The 'p' is very soft, almost silent in fast speech. Sim-tum.
Make sure your teeth touch your bottom lip for the 'V'. Fee-ver.
The 'gh' at the end makes an 'F' sound! Kawf.

expectedPronunciationText: Ache Headache Stomachache Toothache Medicine Pharmacy Hurts Hospital Prescription Symptom Fever Cough

feedbackRules.onPass: Astounding! You successfully navigated some of the hardest spelling traps in English! You pronounced 'Ache' perfectly, with the correct 'K' sound.
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep trying! Remember, 'C-H' in medical words sounds like 'K'. 'Ache' is pronounced 'Ayk'. And 'Cough' is pronounced 'Koff' (with an F sound).
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Watch out for 'Stomachache'. It is long and tricky. Go slowly: Stum-ik-ayk.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.exactCorrectTranscript: ache headache stomachache toothache medicine pharmacy hurts hospital prescription symptom fever cough
acceptedVariants: ache headache stomach ache toothache medicine pharmacy hurts hospital prescription symptom fever cough
adminCorrectAnswerSet.phoneticApproximation: Evaluates phonological decoding of the irregular Greek-origin 'ch' grapheme (/k/ mapping instead of /tʃ/), specifically monitoring vocal production of /eɪk/.

hintText: Whenever you see 'Ache', pretend it is spelled A-K.
commonMistakes: Applying standard English phonics to Greek-origin medical roots (e.g. pronouncing stomach as stomatch).
